svg.style: background-color: #ffffff; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; user-select: none;

g.text: An interactive simulation demonstrating how helicopter cyclic controls manipulate blade pitch,
and how gyroscopic precession causes a 90° phase lag between control input and aerodynamic reaction.
Push the cyclic stick to change the rotor disk angle.
Notice how the blade pitch dynamically changes
as the blades rotate around the mast.
To tilt the helicopter, you need maximum aerodynamic
lift at the opposite end of the desired tilt.
Due to gyroscopic precession, force applied manifests
disk and fuselage up or down.
disk and fuselage side to side.
